entry.title: How to Derive the Duration in Years between 2 dates in SAP Subscription Billing?
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to understand the Duration Calculation component and need assistance with deriving the number of years between two dates.

 

I have configured the system as follows:

  • I am using the Computed Property "Years" as an input to the table. The table data is maintained according to the screenshot below.

     

  • The fields are mapped in "Calculate Price Element" as indicated in the configuration details below.

 

When creating a subscription, I have set the subscription start date to 01.01.2024, but I am receiving a value of 0 in the pricing calculation.

 

Could you please help me understand why this is happening and how I can resolve it?

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Ankit

entry.title: Re: Unable to Rename Apps in SAP Build Apps - Free Edition
entry.content: Perhaps I am missing something ... do you mean this renaming?

entry.title: Re: Deficit of SL Unrestricted-use Message No. M7021
entry.content: Is that a regular inbound delivery or maybe stock transport order or return delivery or ...?

entry.title: Re: Import Fixed asset Master Data From Microsoft Excel
entry.content:
You must have all ACTIVE depreciation areas (COLGAP and NIIF) in your txt file. it's mean you will have two lines to the same item. Also there are  some prerequisites for the different types of assets you want to import. Please, have a look on this documentation: https://help.sap.com/saphelp_sbo900/helpdata/EN/94/7e131c04ca428d933f5375a98aef0e/content.htm?no_cache=true

Also, ensure that the depreciation area ID does not contain any special characters. Use only alphanumeric characters. This was my case the code was "razão" and it supose to be "razao" or any other without special characteres. So, even I've had a unique depreciation area registered it was not recognized from the import file, because it assume you didn't select all the active areas of the asset class.  Unfortunately for me, it was already imported and run some process, so I had to register a ticket with SAP in order to fix it.
TIPS FOR SAP: SAP should not allow the user define any table id/code, and let them personalize their names only.

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition: Guidelines formula
entry.content:
So, you could have the guidelines set with a value which you want to be a percentage. 
For example, below i have 2 for Mentoring and Team Player.

 

Within the business rules on the spotAward object we create the following.

 

We create a variable to get the salary of the employee, have the rule set to always true or for a selected program only, and use the Then to set the award amount to be salary*guideline.
When I tested this on an employee who's salary is 413,000.000 I got 2% correctly.

Hope this helps,
Andres

entry.title: Re: Updating CSV Files in the SAP BI Repository
entry.content: How about saving the files in a shared network-folder and using this as source?

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @AdrianDBorja , Thanks for the feedback.

For 1) massive=True allows to train multiple time series forecast with a single command. In the blog it is used in combination with the group_key to forecast indidvidual values for each country. Maybe you get the error when setting massive=True but without providing that group_key?

For 2) The group_key accepts indeed only a string, not a list or an array. This means you just need to concat the values of the multiple grouping columns together into one column. The name of this new column is then the group_key. Here is an example:

df_remote.select('*', ("REGION || '-' || COUNTRYOFRESIDENCE", 'CONCATCOL'))

For 3) In case you already have a SELECT statement (which might join multiple tables) you can base the hana_ml DataFrame on that custom sql.

df_remote = conn.sql('Your SELECT statement...')

Alternatively you can also use the hana_ml package to join multiple hana_ml DataFrames. Something like:

df_remote = conn.table('USEDCARS').set_index('CAR_ID') \
.join(df_rem_accepted.select(['CAR_ID', 'PREDICTED']) \
.set_index('CAR_ID'), how='left')

For 4) df_rem_countries is created in the Notebook that has all the code. Thanks for letting me know, I will make that clearer in the text

https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-global-center-of-excellence-samples/blob/main/Hands-on%20Tutorial%20Machine%20Learning%20with%20SAP%20HANA%20Cloud/030%20Multiple%20time-series%20forecast.ipynb

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content: @Vitaliy-R this is enterprise acct actually

entry.title: Re: When I move to Quality system from Dev my BW Query OD...

entry.title: Re: Need to Print Smartform on Zebra Bluetooth Printer ZQ320 from Mobile Computer TC58E
entry.content: If it's a custom program, nobody can help here, ask your local developer.

entry.title: Re: Anyone implemented Inspector Qualification?
entry.content: The inspector qualification is rather for documentation purposes. Your use case is not yet entirely clear...where should the check takes place? Brw. there is a similar requirement on the topic on the Customer Influence Portal https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/328791/

entry.title: Carrier Determination - SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 TM
entry.content:
As announced in SAP S/4HANA PcE 2023 FPS03 (https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-fp03-of-transportation-management-in-s-4hana-cloud-private/ba-p/13950181), it is now possible to determine the carrier in the Freight Order, Freight Unit, and Consignment Order at the time of document creation.

This functionality is particularly beneficial in cases where not all advanced TM planning features are utilized. Additionally, it allows users to allocate their time to more critical aspects of the process.

Below I describe a little about it:

  • General Activations and/or Checks
  • Configuration Steps for Functionality Execution
  • Extension
  • New fields in cockpit
  • Carrier propagation

General Activations and/or Checks

1. Freight Unit Stage List, FU/PU Stage List - Field Catalog

2. Freight Unit Stage Hierarchy, FU/PU Stage Hierarchy, Consignment Order List, Consignment Order Hierarchy - Field Catalog

3. Freight Order List, Freight Order Hierarchy, Freight Order / Freight Booking List - Field Catalog

4. Activation of the "Document Mass Change" button - Page layout

5. New Grouping Rule

 

Configuration Steps for Functionality Execution

1. Create Carrier Determination Profile

2. Assign Carrier Determination

Profile in Freight Unit

Freight Order

Consignment Order

If using the Transportation Cockpit and intending to group all demand by "Carrier from Carrier Determination

Create Requirement Grouping Profile

 

Extension

BADI /SCMTMS/EX_TSPD

This BAdI offers three key intervention possibilities:

  • Adjust the carrier determination profile applied to a given business document.
  • Modify the decision on whether redetermination is necessary.
  • Adjust the determined carrier.

 

New Fields in Transportation Cockpit

 

Carrier Propagation

1. Classic Planning: When the Freight Unit (FU) is assigned to a Freight Order (FO), the carrier information is propagated between FU and FO.

2. Multiple Carriers: If two FUs with different carriers exist, the FO will inherit the carrier from the first FU.


3. Post-Planning Adjustments: If an FO is already planned and FUs are removed, the system will retain the carrier from the first FU after modifications.

4. Manual Carrier Assignment: If a carrier is manually populated in the FO, the system will not override it with the FU's carrier information.

entry.title: CSD View - Purchase Order Items Goods Receipt
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I would like to create a CDS view with the “Custom CDS View” app. My goal is to list all purchase order items that are overdue.
I have already found the view I_PurOrdScheduleLineAPI01 to determine the delivery date. However, I am still missing the information as to whether a goods receipt has taken place. Where can I get this from?

Thanks in advance.

 

MM Purchasing Sourcing and Procurement - Purchase Contract Management

entry.title: Re: Bar chart output is in a graded colour
entry.content:
try another color-palette
or
change the formatting of the bar in the chart from automatic to individual and change the color.

entry.title: Customer Hierarchy In Condition Contract

Wanted to know whether we need to have Pricing and Rebates Ticks (Green Check marks) in VDH1N for Hierarchy Nodes, even in S4 Hana, so that rebates pricing can be calculated for Hierarchy node at item level in the Condition contract.

Appreciate your inputs. Thanks in advance.

entry.content:

Where to set email for each user in Fiori?

entry.title: Re: How to share data between a plug-in and the application?
entry.content: Hello Marian,

entry.title: Does anyone have a workaround for null suppression in a waterfall graph?
entry.content:
In SAP Analytics Cloud I want to remove measures from a waterfall that are null. Given filtering on e.g. profit centers, a measure might end up being null and if the filter changes, the measure might take on a positive or negative value and should then be included again.

Datasource is a SAP Datasphere Analytical Model.

Does anyone have a workaround for null suppression in a waterfall graph? As per KBA 3150383 this is not available even though it is possible to enable zero suppression (which does not seem to work either, see screen shots). A couple of screen shots has been attached for context. 

SAC table of datasource without zero or null suppression.

Same table WITH zero and null suppression. All ok.

Waterfall WITH zero suppression. I want to get rid of the highlighted elements. For some reason, Consulting Fee Recovery is not removed with zero suppression.

Thanks for your help.

entry.title: Re: How to share data between a plug-in and the application?
entry.content: Hello Marian, thank you for reading my question. Of course, a fast backend would solve it. But even if it would be fast i would load data twice instead of sharing it between plugin and app. I guess i have to stick with EventHub...

entry.title: Re: Controlling Data Replication by CR Type
entry.content: Use Tcode DRFF and set the filter criteria on Change Request Type

entry.title: SAP HANA Database Server HDB Service Stopping Unexpectedly
entry.content:
Dear SAP Tech users,

For the past few weeks, our SAP HANA DB Server's HDB service has been stopping unexpectedly every other day. To resolve this, I manually log in and run the following command:

/usr/sap/NDB/HDB00/exe/sapcontrol -nr 00 -function RestartSystem HDB
Has anyone else encountered this issue? What could be causing the HDB service to stop, and are there any permanent solutions to prevent this from happening?

entry.title: Re: BW transfer data to 3rd party system Oracle/MSSQL with field value filled with space
entry.content: did you check the transformation-rules in BW from S/4 to BW and also BW to OH ?

entry.title: Re: Using IPS to create a predictable password for non-SS...
entry.content:
Hi

The simplest way to achieve this is what is called "password migration"

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-source-system-to-migrate-user-passwords-from-sap-successfactors-systems-to-identity-authentication

With this scenario your non-SSO will be able to login to SSFF with their old password (SSFF Password) and the passwords will be saved and managed in IAS after the first logon.

Create new passwords and share with the users anyway is not a good idea. As a second option i'd use an activation email if the password synch is not achievable in your scenario.

I'd recommend also to join the call "SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS): Open Office Hours for SAP SuccessFactors HCM". There's one session tomorrow. You'll be able to raise your questions there. Check the links at the top of the blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/migration-to-sap-cloud-identity-authentication-ias-ips-innovation-alert-amp/ba-p/271407

 

Hope it helps

Diego

entry.title: Re: VC Material variants - Multiple Values
entry.content:
Hello,


Did you find any solution to this problem ?

Thank you in advance,

entry.title: SAC Date Calculation in DA
entry.content:
I have a “payment method” dimension with an attribute for “Expected Settlement Time”. Based on the method chosen the attribute can be 1/2/3/4 days (the attribute is set as an integer).

I have a measure for incomes, a measure for outcomes and one created to copy the income in the expected settlement date, named “Expected Outgoing”.

My problem is that I don’t know how to write this calculation - copying the some 3-4 days after based on the attribute.

I’ve tried the below, but the syntax isn’t correct, as it mixes date and number of days:

DATA([d/Measures] = "Expected_Outgoing") = 
RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Income", [d/Date] = DATEDIFF( [d/Date],[d/IL_D_POC_RPD_PMNTMTHD].[p/STLMNT_TIME],"DAY" ) )

DATA([d/Measures] = "Expected_Outgoing") =

RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Income", [d/Date] = DATEDIFF( [d/Date], [d/IL_D_POC_RPD_PMNTMTHD].[p/STLMNT_TIME], "DAY" ) )

 

How should I approach that?

entry.title: Re: Fiori: where to set email for user
entry.content:
Hi,

You can administer the email-adress in transaction SU01.

Nils

entry.title: Error while uploading Job Req data via integration center
entry.content:
Hi,

When trying to import data in Job Requisition via integration center, getting errors in integration center job

Error 1.JobRequisition: Mandatory value is missing for deleted for record jobReqId-we have got the values whether deleted or not deleted.

Error 2- when maintained data and trying to run the job getting another error -JobRequisition: UPSERT failed for the row with key: . Reason: Field deleted not valid for templateId id, . The row will not be processed for subsequent entities.

Upon checking we can see there is no field in template.

entry.title: Collective order in SAP - target and actual costs
entry.content:
Hi All,

 

I have a scenario, where I have order with couple of suborders. Confirmation is done by external MES system. Each of suborders are confirmed separately via MES. Control key in routings set with automatic GR. Coll. order with goods movement is activated in cost controlling in my production order type. While confirming  the suborder 101 movement is done and 261. 

The problem is between the end of the month and the beginning of the other one. Imagine the situation when all of suborders are confirmed in one month and the main order is confirmed in the next month. Then while having cost report in kkbc_ord (limited to the previous month) I cannot see the target's cost in suborders. There are 'artificial' variances, which will disappear after confirming the order in next month.

Is there any chance to have these target costs in suborders? 

Regards, thank you for your help!

entry.title: The ABAP Detective and the Errant Electrolyte
entry.content:
An aspect of sustainability is minimal resource use, in both the sense of lower costs, but also the avoidance of energy use that contributes to carbon dioxide emissions from fossil fuels. For an establishment on "all-electric" the latter is what might be called an externality. As a code and systems analysis detective, I look for areas of improvement.

Previous investigations into environmental monitoring tools are noted at the end; this case focuses on the efficiency and use of domestic hot water delivery. In the backwaters of the united states, standard appliances include a 30 or 40 gallon (sorry: X liters) tank with a heating source constantly attempting to maintain a set minimum temperature. Other areas use the "on-demand" approach, or hot-water-as-a-service, with a heating device close to the delivery point (let's not go into the 110 versus 220 volt infrastructure as a distraction).

To the crux of the biscuit: I set up a temperature monitor directly above the output of the hot water tank, connected to a Raspberry Pi that is polled by a central system. Other approaches would be locally collecting the data and uploading in scheduled bursts, pushing rather than pulling.

TMP102 gauge, copper, insulation. (Photo by Jim Spath)

(photo by Jim Spath)

This ad hoc wiring approach works in a non-intrusive way, as the sensor unit does not touch the copper pipes, nor contact running water. Typically throughout the day, hot water is not running, so the readings are close to ambient room temperature. When a tap is opened somewhere, preheated water goes near enough to tick up the temperature reading, then after the flows stops the temperature gradually degrades. Or, if the tank completely empties of heated water, the readings will correspondingly decline.

Standalone sensor:

TMP102 gauge with blips

(screenshot from Zabbix web client)

A few times during the day, temperature rises, then declines. Not a particularly useful data set if you want to ask questions like "how hot is the hot water" or "how long did the washer run?" or even "how much did this unit of work cost?". The human brain can glance at these shapes and discern patterns, like Sherlock Holmes checking out cigar-ash-flakes in a pursuit. Writing an algorithm is a bit trickier.

The Zabbix open source suite I'm using has tools to run calculations on data feeds; for instance in some cases I'd pull values in Celsius, then add a derived parameter converting to Fahrenheit. There are ways to "look back" to prior values to determine deltas (which I've not implemented yet). You can also set up triggers to perform actions such as alerting on worst case scenarios (like overheating or frozen pipes: Ow!).

I'll gloss over the gnarly details of the Sparkfun/QWIIC monitoring hardware/software and cut to the chase: only one specific gauge per Pi, without adding multiplexing I2C complexities, so I was faced with deploying 2 Pi's with the same gauge type, or add a different gauge on 1 Pi. I chose the latter. Then the case went into what we colloquially called a "mung" state. Not "hung" but not right either.

Pi and the OS

If I could, I'd "flame" here, showing the rapid pace of software development breaks things that worked a few short years ago in surprising ways. I'm using code that someone else wrote in bash shell scripting back in 2012 or so, and that logic doesn't need to be tweaked when a new Linux kernel or even a subsystem component is written. And generally, Python examples the vendors share keeps working from year to year. Upgrades to the running environment are non-breaking other than minor steps like "oh, use Python 3.12 now instead of 3.10" that are easy enough to resolve.

My first Pi platform had a "6.1" 32-bit Linux kernel version; more recently new installs have a "6.6" kernel, where 64-bit flavors are as stable as the 32-bit. Great, why would that matter? Somewhere in the matrix, Python grew an appendage, nicknamed "venv" meaning a virtual environment. Let's add containers, crates, and virtualization while we're at it. Except this is a $10 machine with a single purpose.

TMP102 and BME280

These 2 temperature sensors are available in the chosen "ecosystem" that connects the Pi brain to the digital sensors, which could also include humidity and barometric pressure. Since I had collected and deployed at least one of each, I assumed connecting both to 1 Pi would be simple. I had code samples in shell and Python for both. "Just" follow the manufacturers instructions and "pip" it into place (no, not the old DEC PIP peripheral to peripheral connector, the new python software package installer). Failure to launch:

 

$ pip install qwiic_bme280
error: externally-managed-environment

 

Oh, darn, this didn't work, and spit out a bunch of scary messages, especially this last dooms-day-scenario:

 

You can override this, at the risk of breaking \
 your Python installation or OS,  \
 by passing --break-system-packages. \
hint: See PEP 668 for the detailed specification.

 

"Break system packages" sounds ominous. Maybe don't take that approach. 

pip breakage

(screenshot from terminal session)

A more staid detective would go read the reference manuals, consult others online, re-read the source code, then diligently make necessary repairs. Others more reactive might literally check with their vendors and ask what's up with this. Investigators with a hacking tendency would start typing likely fixes and rout/route around the damage. Command line use of a venv turned out to be fairly simple:

 

python3 -m venv venv
venv/bin/pip3 install sparkfun-qwiic-bme280

[...]
Successfully installed \
 smbus2-0.5.0 \
 sparkfun-qwiic-bme280-0.9.0 \
 sparkfun-qwiic-i2c-1.0.0

 

No doubt more experienced Python developers are aghast at the hammer approach I used.

Getting readings at the command line would now work with 2 sensors; the new one shows:

 

$ venv/bin/python3.11  /path/to/sparkfun.py  temperature_celsius
16.2118

 

Fine, a 3-pipe problem rather than a trivial 2-pipe one. Except the whole intent is to automate sensor data collection, not have to run a command line manually. I'll leave out the messy trial-and-error details, other than to share that the Zabbix process debug steps boil down to seeing whether a parameter shows as "not supported" or returns an error message such as "got string, expecting a number" in the console, and checking the agent logs for clues (like: "The Qwiic BME280 device isn't connected to the system. Please check your connection" when I used the wrong sensor).

Stuffed inside an old shell script are new links to the monitor logic.

 

/path/venv/python3 /path/sparkfun-venv.py temperature_celsius

 

The middle section didn't need to have any virtual environment goo but for the sake of my future self looking at these sensors after the next incompatibility will possibly recall why.

Before going to the trouble of writing calculation or trigger steps in parameter definitions I used a handy UI feature in Zabbix that overlays 2 measurements in one chart. First, make sure both metrics are stable:

2 charts

(screenshot from Zabbix web client)

Then, add the second parameter to a dashboard widget. I didn't muck around with colors, lines, or transparencies, taking the defaults as good enough for the quick quality control / QC check.

2 temperature sensors on 1 chart

(screenshot from Zabbix web client)

Next problems to solve?

  • The 2 gauges differ under "no flow" states by over 1 degree.
  • Alerts/triggers
  • Validation/calibration

Calibration

Back in the last century, my polytechnic/polymath education included calibrating thermometers by determining how close their readings were to freezing boiling water temperatures. A memorable lesson in theory versus practice, tossing aside the novice assumption that a device always shows accurate values. How many degrees or tenths-of-degrees plus or minus the correct reading? (let's skip over side issues such as does water boil at exactly 100 degrees Celsius at any specific elevation or location).

What I'd probably investigate next is recording the temperature at the taps compared to the utility room infrastructure, particularly after hearing anecdotal comments such as "the kitchen sink faucet is not hot enough."

NOTES / REFERENCES

Shell:

  • https://znil.net/index.php/Zabbix:Template_Raspberry_Pi
  • https://znil.net/index.php?title=Temperatur_/_Spannung_etc._des_Raspberry_Pi_selbst_auslesen

Python:

  • http://rptl.io/venv
  • https://docs.python.org/3/library/venv.html
  • https://peps.python.org/pep-0668/
  • https://github.com/sparkfun/qwiic_bme280_py

SAP Community posts:

  • The ABAP Detective Takes The Summer Heat
  • The ABAP Detective Gets Their Clock Cleaned
  • The ABAP Detective Takes The Heat, or How To Cleanse Data

Also:

  • Intra home detectors

entry.title: No Work Orders Downloaded
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to implement SSAM 2410 and have reviewed lots of documentation but still unable to download work orders and notifications to mobile app (Functional Locations and Equipment have downloaded successfully, 'out of box')

I have set the following for work orders

Perona - Maintenance Tech (Pro)

OMDO  Filter - SAM2410_Order_Type with category, order type and planning plant

OMDO  Filter - SAM2410_Work_Order_Generic with main wc, ord type, planning Plant PM_Phase, WO assignment Type.

Have tried both Handler and Userparam rules

What am I missing?

Haven't touched Notification ODMO settings yet but out of box is also not working.

Thanks in advance for any suggestions.

entry.title: WBS Authorizations UE
entry.content:
I want to use UE for WBS Authorizations - I've implemented : FUNCTION EXIT_SAPLCNAU_002.

When I change WBS it doesn't go through this function.

When I debug it I see that it happens because of this line:

Meaning flg_exit_active-cnau_002 is empty.

How may I make it non empty please?

Thanks

Noy

entry.title: Standard Content in SEN for SAP RAR module
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there standard content available for RAR module in SAP Enable Now?

Best,

Ajith K J

entry.title: One Network, Many Journeys
entry.content:
SAP Business Network began as a procurement platform – still its leading force today - then slowly started touching on additional operations. Today, the network includes supply chain, logistic and asset management transactions. As a result, the types of trading partners playing a role on the network flourished beyond the typical supplier-buyer relationship. Millions of trading partners interact daily in this constantly evolving environment, and not everyone had the same journey of joining SAP Business Network.

For organizations in need of a more holistic view of the network, we have put together a summarizing presentation exploring diverse journeys and multiple potential access points that suppliers and trading partners take to join and use SAP Business Network.

Click on the image to open the document

This deck is designed to provide a broad overview and valuable insights of how trading partners collaborate on SAP Business Network, highlighting key touchpoints and engagements with our teams.


We hope you’ll find this document helpful to map out your past, present and future journeys on SAP Business Network.

entry.title: Re: Best Practices for SAP HANA DB Installation on SUSE L...
entry.content: no, SAPB1 on HANA requires SLD services to be installed on Linux OS.

entry.title: Re: Deficit of SL Unrestricted-use Message No. M7021
entry.content: Just a regular inbound delivery from a PO

entry.title: Story data dictionary (Not the old learning one)
entry.content:
Hi

I'm looking for the Story Data Dictionary please if anyone can help. So I can find fields as my search in Stories isn't working. I just want to be able to find fields without spending hours looking manually through tables.

I'm not referring to the Learning one as that's not for stories as the table names are very different. Although it would be good to have a learning story one as well for the future.

Thanks

entry.title: Selling projects to leaders
entry.content:
Let’s start with one thing, if you struggle to sell the benefits of a good E-Commerce strategy to your Senior Leadership Team, you should quit your job, as the company won’t last long. However, there are some ways to enhance the role that E-Commerce can play in your business by getting the full backing of your superiors.

It’s no secret that Directors and Executives are interested in two major factors; Return on Investment and Client Satisfaction. E-Commerce professionals are very lucky that a well-developed strategy will cater for both of these in bucket loads. Automating your interactions with clients can cut the cost of transaction by up to two thirds, whilst also improving your clients’ experience of trading with you.

If I could offer one piece of advice, it would be to develop your skills in writing and presenting business cases. There is a lot to be said for taking the time to create a well prepared slide deck with all the relevant stats, expected ROI and time frames. Also, if the recent UK General Election has taught us anything, it’s that we need to fully cost our proposals before presenting them! Nothing is going to cut your presentation to a CEO or President short like not being able to directly answer their question. Do your research, prepare your slides, then present positively and confidently. Passion and exuberance are contagious and a vital part of your armory when requesting budget. Mentoring can also provide valuable input when preparing a business case. Is there someone you look up to within your company or a former boss that you maintain a great relationship with? If so, use them as a sounding board. Sometimes, there is no substitute for experience and great leaders love nothing more than having the opportunity to share that experience to help others.

Returning to my earlier point, Client Satisfaction goes hand in hand with ROI. The former can have a hugely positive impact on the latter if it’s done right. One phrase should be at the forefront of any good E-Commerce strategy; ‘How can I remove all obstacles to my clients’ ability to purchase?’ We only get two chances to impress prospective clients. Just two negative experiences will lead to you losing the client, probably forever. This is a small margin of error, so put yourself in your clients’ shoes from day one. Your customers need to be central to your thinking when designing and developing your E-Commerce offerings. Don’t forget that one of your best marketing tools is satisfied clients spreading word of your value. A successful leadership team will value and appreciate this methodology, making them far more likely to buy in to your way of thinking.

You also have one major advantage when approaching your bosses for a new E-Commerce project. Automating client experience reduces the cost and time of transaction, allowing your sales teams more time to be productive. E-Commerce teams typically run very lean and can deliver tangible benefits which are plain for all to see. Clients who integrate their ERPs with your business are also more patient if you make mistakes. A typical integration project can take up to three months (more for a large global client). This isn’t a cycle that businesses like to go through more often than they have to. Treat these clients well and you should be able to expand your sales portfolio with them, over a relationship lasting years.

Ultimately, you need to accept a leadership role yourself by driving E-Commerce within your business. Senior leaders rely on subject matter experts to steer their decision making. Guide them well, then deliver on your commitments!

Before approaching your leadership team with new ideas, keep the below in mind:

· Practice writing business cases

· Prepare and know your subject

· Be passionate

· Remove all the client’s obstacles

· Provide ROI

· Follow up and deliver

entry.title: Re: Deficit of SL Unrestricted-use Message No. M7021
entry.content: What Is Special About This Inbound Delivery?Has This Worked Before?Is the Item Category Correct?

entry.title: Fill Parameter Year in Data Action using setParameterValue
entry.content:
Hi,

i'm facing an issue trying to pass a parameter in a data action using setParameterValue. I need to pass a year, as you can do it with the data action trigger.

I've seen that a similar problem has already been discussed, but in my situation, the solution proposed it's not working: 
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sac-fill-parameter-year-in-data-action-using-analytic-app-script/qaq-p/12606009

Am i missing something?

Kind regards,

Enrico

entry.title: Re: No Work Orders Downloaded
entry.content: Ensure that the OMDO filters are correctly assigned to the Persona and the user has the required authorization roles. Check if Mobile Data Object (MDO) sync rules are correctly configured for work orders. Also, validate the ODMO settings for notifications and verify if the Sync Criteria in MDK metadata allows downloading work orders and notifications.

entry.title: Re: Collective order in SAP - target and actual costs
entry.content:
In a collective order scenario, target costs are typically calculated at the header level, and suborders inherit costs upon final confirmation of the main order.
Since your main order is confirmed in the next month, the system does not distribute target costs to suborders in the previous month's KKBC_ORD report,
leading to temporary variances.

To see target costs in suborders, you can try:
1. Early settlement of suborders before month-end to reflect costs properly.
2. Using statistical cost reporting for suborders in KKBC_ORD.
3. Checking Cost Rollup settings in Costing Variant to enable cost distribution at suborder level.

entry.title: Re: Opening transaction in SAPGUI for HTML via URL fails...
entry.content:
@tobias_grimm_ksg - Try with this instead:

https://YOURSERVER.com/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/shells/abap/FioriLaunchpad.html?flp#QualityNotification-display?sap-ui-tech-hint=GUI&QualityNotification=000230000001

Hope this helps. /Daniel

entry.title: Re: Selling projects to leaders
entry.content:
A strong argument for strategic E-Commerce management! 💡 It’s undeniable that a company’s success today heavily depends on how effectively it leverages digital sales channels—not just for revenue growth but also for long-term customer retention.

One of the key points you highlight is the ability to clearly communicate ROI. Executives need solid numbers and compelling business cases to approve investments. Those who come prepared don’t just secure budgets—they position themselves as strategic thinkers.

I particularly appreciate your point about automation: Efficient digital processes reduce costs, enhance customer experience, and free up sales teams to focus on high-value tasks. Companies that fail to capitalize on this advantage risk falling behind the competition.

Ultimately, driving a successful E-Commerce strategy means taking ownership and seeing oneself as a leader—an often underestimated aspect. Those who present well-researched, practical solutions with confidence and passion will win leadership buy-in.

A great post with valuable, real-world insights!

entry.title: Re: How to build a responsive UI for a RAP business object that starts an asynchronous process
entry.content:
@Andre_Fischer Thank you for sharing... curious to know, Do we need to raise an event manually like seen in video (button click), is it possible we do similar to APC/MPC which helps updating UI automatically as soon as backend data changes...

Thanks-

entry.title: Re: Things to know when using SAP_CUST client copy profile
entry.content:
I think, the problem about MDG staging tables is actually solved with the 3253943 - SCCL(N) with profile SAP_CUST copies MDG application tables too.

We also faced this problem after local client copy with Profile SAP_UCUS.

entry.title: Post Acquisition Integrated Using the Manage Supplier Invoices (F0859)
entry.content:
Hello,

According to the information found in the SAP help area, the F0859 app can be used to record supplier invoices for the purchase of assets without a purchase order, however, we did not find the necessary fields to include the relevant information of the asset already created.
We are using the Manage Supplier Invoices (F0859) app for workflow processing of invoices. SAP S4/HANA Private edition.
Please advise.

entry.title: Re: openSAP Microlearning videos are now bite-sized learning videos on the SAP Learning site
entry.content:
@sshen-illumiti It is not possible to download the videos. 

Since you are interested in SAP Graph, I recommend to check out this site too https://developers.sap.com/tutorial-navigator.html?tag=software-product-function%3Asap-hana-graph

entry.title: Re: How to Delete Active ML Records in SAP?
entry.content: I don't think so you can deactivate ML without deleting historical data for that Plant/CC. ML can only be deactivated if there is no historical/transactional data. Please raise an Incident with SAP and maybe they can help you.

entry.title: E-Learning for SAP LeanIX Application Ownership Model is available.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Signavio Community, 

SAP Business Transformation Management Value Map, along with the SAP LeanIX Academy team, is excited to announce we have released our E-Learning sessions for SAP LeanIX Application Ownership Model.  This E-Learnings is hosted on the SAP Enterprise Support Academy.

What the sessions is about:

Learn how to implement a proper application ownership model.  This micro-learning course enables you to better understand who the responsible parties are, how to identify them, and what options are available to get the relevant data into SAP LeanIX

 What are the session goals?

  • Understand what an application ownership model is
  • Understand the concept of application ownership
  • Understand the role and related tasks of an application owner
  • Understand the two different personas: application business owner and application technology owner (IT)
  • Learn how to identify owners and add them to the workspace

How to access?

NOTE:  You MUST be registered with the SAP Enterprise Support Academy in order to access the “direct links” to these courses below (and can do so right here if not currently registered).

Get started with SAP Enterprise Support Academy and Value Maps here. (Check out our step-by-step instructional video  on how to join SAP Enterprise Support Academy and Value maps.)

Direct link to the new E-Learning courses (once registered as stated above).

  • Course: SAP LeanIX:  Application Ownership Model

Do you need additional help?

Don't hesitate to get in touch with our team using the following form. (Please select the "Business Transformation Management" topic).

Regards, 

Leean De Loria

Our System are missing the SAP predelivered content and I don't know what to update here. In the best of worlds I would be able to fetch from 000 client but this is not possible. Is there anywere I can find this content.

entry.title: Re: Using IPS to create a predictable password for non-SS...
entry.content: Thanks Diego for the suggestions, but the Password Migration only works one time and not for ongoing adding of new synced users. Unless I am misunderstanding this is a one time activity, and leaving the IPS code in will give your users password issues. As stated above, the users do not have emails to use the activation emails. Thanks I will try to join soon and raise the question as it is becoming more common scenario.

entry.title: Re: Using IPS to create a predictable password for non-SS...
entry.content: Thanks Diego for the suggestions, but the Password Migration only works one time and not for ongoing adding of new synced users. Unless I am misunderstanding this is a one time activity, and leaving the IPS code in will give your users password issues. As stated above, the users do not have emails to use the activation emails. Thanks I will try to join soon and raise the question as it is becoming more common scenario.

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 app index.html as a landing page on SAPGUI home screen
entry.content: HI Marian,Yes we can use a service user authentication. But we wanted to see if SSO can be accomplished here as the app is also on the same server.

entry.title: Msg No. M8534
entry.content:
HI

Balance not zero: 1.050.000,000-  debits: 1.050.000,000  credits: 0,000

Message No. M8534

Diagnosis

The system has discovered a difference between the debits and credits. This difference lies outside the tolerance limits set.

System Response

The system cannot post the document.

Procedure

If this message appears when you are processing a document online, you can change the amounts or enter new items until you can post the document.

You can change the Invoice Verification type, so that the document can be posted later in the background. If this message appeared when a document was verified in the background, you can now process the document.

entry.title: Budget Values showing different values in executive review & exported Bonus payout summary report
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are getting different values in Executive Review for Budget values and if we export Bonus payout summary report from executive review in Variable Pay Template.

However, if I export the data and sum the Budget column, we see   €1,099,373.89. 

Can you please help us in understanding different behavior of the budget and which values we should refer.

@xavierlegarrec 

Regards,

Satish

entry.title: Embracing the Future: Chatbots, Copilots, and Agents in Procurement
entry.content:
One of my earliest memories of advanced technology involves voice recognition software. I was home from college, getting ready to fly back to school, when I overheard my dad on the phone. He was struggling to communicate with the airline’s automated system. He repeated my airline's name twice, carefully enunciated my flight number, and then, in frustration, shouted "Operator!" multiple times.

Since then, we’ve moved from automated systems through the phone to in-home virtual assistants like Siri, Google or Alexa. These advancements have made our lives significantly easier, and they continue to evolve.

Technology has grown by leaps and bounds – and it’s not just limited to voice recognition. It’s also playing a crucial role in simplifying complex procurement processes and enhancing user experiences. Today, voice recognition is just one of the many tools helping to automate and streamline operations.

Among the most transformative technologies are AI-assisted chatbots, copilots, and agents, which are revolutionizing how procurement professionals interact with systems and data.

Chatbots have been around for a while. They started off by handling basic queries, but their capabilities have expanded significantly with the advent of generative AI (GenAI). Now, chatbots can understand and process natural language, which makes each interaction more intuitive.

Copilots offer sophisticated, conversational capabilities. They act as strategic assistants and help procurement professionals navigate complex workflows and make informed decisions based on business context.

Agents are designed to autonomously perform complex, multistep workflows. They use AI to plan, reason, and execute tasks, allowing procurement teams to increase activity levels while also focusing on strategic objectives.

How You Can Use Chatbots and Copilots

In procurement, chatbots can guide users through procurement processes with predefined rules. They often provide standard responses to frequently asked questions on policies, processes, and procedures.

SAP's copilot, Joule, integrates seamlessly across SAP applications, including SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass solutions, to enhance procurement operations and leverage business context with historical data to guide users conversationally. Joule can also help with tasks like approvals, running reports, and answering questions like:

  • Which suppliers have responded to our sourcing event?
  • Where are the time sheets I need to approve? 
  • Who is the final approver for my high-profile project?

In addition to improving productivity, these technologies offer multiple benefits for procurement, such as:

  • Enhanced user experience. Intelligence is directly embedded into the user interface, ensuring interactions are part of everyday activities.
  • Streamlined processes. Automation of tedious and complex tasks like generating RFxs makes it easier to ensure compliance and maximize efficiency.
  • Better supplier management. Advanced insights help teams manage supplier performance data to reduce risks and strengthen critical relationships.

The Future of Agents

In many ways, the frustration my dad experienced with that early voice recognition system feels like a distant memory.  Today, we have numerous apps to help track flights. However, in some ways, the frustrations still feel relevant because some of these systems remain a work in progress. Regardless, it’s clear that technology is advancing with a focus on making it more intuitive and user-friendly.

Agents will revolutionize procurement by autonomously executing complex, multistep workflows and accelerating cross-functional operations, which will continuously run and ultimately free up resources. These agents will work alongside you and your colleagues, ensuring collaboration and oversight while enhancing efficiency and accuracy in procurement processes.

As these technologies evolve, they will transform procurement from a process-driven function to a strategic enabler.

Take the Next Step

Learn more about how these innovations are shaping the future of procurement. Join us for our March 27 webinar* with Pierre Mitchell, chief research officer at Spend Matters, to explore the potential ways you can use chatbots, copilots, and agents to drive procurement excellence in your organization.

Register today 

*This webinar will be available in multiple time zones.

entry.title: Re: Screen Personas Slipstream engine – Footer Buttons no...
entry.content:
Hi Vananko I didn't realize that if I was using the Slipstream engine, I would also have to create the flavor there. Makes perfect sense of course! I am still a bit confused about the terms status bar and toolbar, and why it appears at the bottom of the mobile view, but it worked 😊.

I have now moved 'Save' and 'Cancel' from the bottom to the top of the desktop view.

In the mobile version they are now at the bottom, which is what I wanted.

 

Is there any trick I can use to test this directly on the desktop? When I try it in the Developer Tool, it is still the 'desktop' view, even if I select iPhone, for example. Best Regards Simon

entry.title: Re: Fiori: where to set email for user
entry.content:
Are you asking for an SAP user email address or an HR employee's personal email address?

  • SU01 for users master records
  • Maintain Employees

entry.content: The message is clear, isn't it? You are not posting any credits.

entry.title: Re: Screen Personas Slipstream engine – Footer Buttons no...
entry.content: After device is selected in Developer Tools you have to reload page to load mobile version.

entry.title: Msg No. SG105
entry.content:
HI EVERYONE

Enter rate INR / EUR rate type MCA for 26.02.2025 in the system settings

Message No. SG105

Diagnosis

For the conversion of an amount into another currency, an entry is missing in the currency conversion table.

Procedure

Add the missing entry in the currency conversion table.

entry.title: Re: Fiori: where to set email for user
entry.content: @raymond_giuseppi Anywhere that will reflect Fiori user profile.

entry.title: Change status and content of standard field 'Purchase requisition description'
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

currently, the "Purchase Requisition Description" field is automatically prefilled with the requestor’s name and the PR request date.
We would like to make this field mandatory (with red asterisk, same as field document type), and also blank by default, so the requisitioner is forced to write more detailed description.

We have checked custom logics for:
- changing field status ( MM_PUR_S4_PR_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE) but description field is not available in the interface of this logic, 
- changing content of the field to be blank (MM_PUR_S4_PR_MODIFY_ITEM), but also, field is not available in the interface (PR does not have a header line, so I checked this badi).

Is it even possible to change status and content of Purchase Requisition description field? 

My question is related to app Manage purchase requisition - professional (F2229) for public cloud.

Thank you very much for all your help
Veronika

entry.title: Re: How to share data between a plug-in and the application?
entry.content:
you can just put it under window object

like window.__uniquenameforyourdata

entry.title: Re: SAP Screen Personas 3.0 SP18 Flavor freezes
entry.content:
These errors are not related to Personas.

If problem happens while using onAfterRefresh script, you have to examine. Scripting just replicates user input. So read your script and try to repeat everything manually. This could give you the idea what doesn't work and if there is some problem in product or some situation is not handled correctly in your script.

entry.title: Re: F4 help for Application Job in Public Cloud (2502 release)
entry.content:
Hi @abhishekd ! 

Thanks for the blog post! I tried using a standard search help(I_DISTRIBUTIONCHANNELVALUEHELP) like this and got this error 

Object I_DISTRIBUTIONCHANNELVALUEHELP must not be used (due to ABAP language version)

My ABAP language verion is ABAP for Cloud Development

What's even more surprising is that it gives me the same error on my own custom CDS views that I created similar to yours.

Any clues? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: What is all about Authorization Server (OAuth) SSO introduced in SAP BusinessObjects BI 4.3 SP02
entry.content:
Hi Venkat
Hope all is well,

External reverse proxy with Apache between SAP BO application and on-premises IDP . The dispatch URL is running into reverse proxy apache and it connects to IDP using WebAgent for authentication and reponse token shared through trusted header (HTTP_HEADER and UID) . trying to migrate WebAgent  to Mod auth OIDC . Reverse Apache exchanges the OIDC token back to SAP BO applicaiton and can't understand by SAP BO application to provide user access. Even we have added authorization server configuration and enabled openid connect Authentication is enabled. What configuration missing in our end.

entry.title: Re: Using IPS to create a predictable password for non-SS...
entry.content:
Hi,

Leaving the code in IPS will not cause an issue. This will be checked just once on the first login of the user and password then managed in IAS (if needed).


Technically you can keep that config forever in IPS and the new users in SSFF with loginmethod=PWD will be able to do the same. i.e use the password you set in SSFF initially. Once the user logins (and maybe change the pwd depending on the config you set up future local PWD changes have to be managed directly from IAS.


Clearly that's not the normal use case scenario and probably not intended by SAP to be used but if you already had a way to setup passwords for these new users in SSFF you can still do so and new users will be able to use initially the pwd you set in SSFF.


Also If these new users you are mentioned are in another IDP that supports SAML/OIDC you can add multiple IDPs to IAS and send the users to different IDPs based on rules

entry.title: Re: Collective order in SAP - target and actual costs
entry.content: Thank you so much!

entry.title: Re: Collective order in SAP - target and actual costs

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Manage Situation object, Manage Situation - ex...
entry.content: @Mandy2025Could you please try assigning the SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT role to your user?

entry.title: Generate intercompany billing request based on the results of running allocation cycles (S4H Public)
entry.content:
We have a scenario where the parent company incurs shared costs (e.g., IT, HR, facility expenses) and allocates them to a subsidiary using intercompany cost allocation cycles (app Manage allocation cycles).

  • There is no time reporting, no activity types, and no end user reporting hours.
  • Only IC cost allocations are done via allocation cycles Manage Allocation cycles app  (e.g., from one cost center to another across company codes).
  • We need to generate an intercompany invoice (Intercompany billing request) based on these allocations.
  • We need to add also margin to such intercompany invoices

We understand that there is only one solution to activate 4AN, 4AU, 16T scope items that relates to professional services industry. 4AN includes the proces J54 (overhead accounting) that allows to generat intercompany billing based on app Reassign cost/revenues. We have tested this process in SAP demo. 

As a result, the Intercompany Billing Request was successfully generated for this posting (via Reassign cost/revenues app) but ignored the results of Manage allocation cycles app althow we used the same GL account, the same Sender and Receiver. 

 The difference between these two transactions lies in the Business Transaction Types: ACAC (automatic allocation) and KAMV (manual allocation). It appears that KAMV works as expected, but ACAC does not. 

We need confirmation on whether an Intercompany Billing Request (ICBR) can be generated from cost allocation cycles in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Specifically:

  1. Does the system support generating an ICBR based on allocation cycles (e.g., cost center-to-cost center allocations across company codes)?

    • Please confirm only if you are sure this works.
    • Kindly do not reference standard 4AN (Reassign Cost and Revenue) documentation/blogs unless it explicitly confirms allocation cycles trigger intercompany billing.

  2. How can we add a margin to the intercompany invoice if we do not have activity types?

  3. If SAP does not support this process in Public Cloud, what is the recommended approach to achieve this?

We appreciate any clear guidance based on actual system behavior.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Add filters to Talent Pools Succession
entry.content:
Dear Max and Anastasia,

I am looking for the same thing, can we extend the list of fields from "Manage Talent Pool Field Settings' to other UDF fields? We have the same requirement at my current project. 

Unfortunately the links provided by you Max are no longer working. 

And given Anastasia's last post, It doesn't seem clear if there is a standard solution to add any other fields as possible for selection criteria.

Many thanks in advance!

Carla

entry.title: Re: Msg No. SG105
entry.content: Mute, no context and no question, consider improving your questioning skills. Anyway, already answered here: https://www.google.com/search?q=SG105+site%3Asap.com

entry.title: DRF IDOC Errors
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,
Hope all are doing good.
I'm currently working on implementing Data Replication Framework (DRF) in SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) for material data replication between two systems:

Sender: SAP S/4HANA MDG 1909
Receiver: SAP S/4HANA MDG 2020

I've completed the following IDoc configuration

Sender System (1909)
1. Configured Logical System (SALE)
2. Assigned Logical System (SALE)
3. Set up RFC Destination (SM59)
4. Configured Port (WE21)
5. Created Distribution Model (BD64)
6. Generated Partner Profile
7. Distribute the Model View

Receiver System (2020)
1. Configured Logical System (SALE)
2. Assigned Logical System (SALE)
3. In Distribution Model Create Partner Profile (BD64)


After completing these configurations, I tested the setup using transaction code 'BD10' For Material the IDoc status in the sender system shows success. However, although data is being received in the receiver system, it's not being created.

I've attached the error details for your reference. Could you please help me troubleshoot this issue?

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: Optimizing Performance in SAP Commerce Cloud: Understanding Autoscaling
entry.content: How can I check if autoscaling is enabled/disabled in one of the Production services? SAP told my project that one of our Production services has disabled autoscaling. Can I confirm this myself in Cloud Portal or HAC? If yes, how? Or, do I need to ask SAP to get this information?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS3: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry?
entry.content:
Hi @michael_ernzerhoff ,

thanks for the update.

Are the APIs available on https://api.sap.com/ already, and if yes. Can you drop a link? 

I was not able to find them in https://api.sap.com/package/S4HANAOPAPIUTL/overview 

 

Kind regards

Till

entry.title: Re: Msg No. M8534
entry.content:
Sometimes you may need to delete unused items from the invoice to avoid a false error message. To do this, click on the "Delete Unselected Item" button at the bottom of the MIRO screen (it looks like the sync icon).

Of course, I assume that you have already entered the vendor/vat amounts in the header and selected some non-zero items.

entry.title: Re: Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
entry.content:
@nageshcaparthy 

Hi, 

Get token is not available for customizing tenant , any reason and how to fix it?

thankyou

ebsor
